findings.threshold: Horizontal scaling via replica addition + DAPR actor placement (consistent hashing)
findings.description: DAPR actor placement infrastructure is implicitly present (AggregateActor uses DAPR actor framework, which relies on the placement service for consistent-hash distribution across replicas). The Aspire AppHost (src/Hexalith.EventStore.AppHost/Program.cs) wires the DAPR sidecar per project but does NOT call .WithReplicas(...) or otherwise configure replica count -- single-instance topology by default. No test runs two or more eventstore replicas and verifies actor redistribution or state cohesion across instances. Deployment guides reference HPA/KEDA (documented only, not configured).
evidence: src/Hexalith.EventStore.AppHost/Program.cs (no WithReplicas call -- single instance by default)
recommendations: Add Tier 3 Aspire test that runs 2+ eventstore replicas and validates actor placement reshuffling on replica addition/removal
Provide reference HPA/KEDA manifests under deploy/ (currently only in docs)

findings.threshold: >= 10,000 active aggregates per instance without latency degradation
findings.description: No volume/scale test exists. Glob for Volume*, LoadScale*, 10K*, 1000Aggregate* returned zero matches. SnapshotRehydrationTests only exercises single-aggregate paths with <= 55 events and 10 events in the baseline cases. The 10K active-aggregate claim is entirely unvalidated -- neither memory footprint, actor-activation throughput, nor latency are measured at that scale.
recommendations: Add a benchmark/volume harness that activates 10,000 distinct aggregate actors and measures p50/p99 command latency plus memory footprint
Capture baseline per-aggregate memory cost to size instance RAM
Gate release on the 10K aggregate benchmark meeting the NFR17 target

findings.threshold: >= 10 tenants per instance with full isolation, no cross-tenant interference
findings.description: Tenant isolation is FULL at the routing/ActorId level (MultiTenantRoutingIntegrationTests verifies that {tenant}:{domain}:{aggregateId} ActorIds are unique per tenant; MultiTenantIsolationTests and ActorTenantIsolationTests exercise same-aggregateId-across-tenants isolation). However, no test exercises 10+ concurrent tenants driving commands simultaneously to assert no interference under load -- the 'no cross-tenant interference' clause of NFR18 is asserted structurally (via ActorId uniqueness) but not empirically under concurrent multi-tenant traffic.
recommendations: Extend MultiTenantRoutingIntegrationTests to 10+ tenants with concurrent command dispatch (Parallel.ForEachAsync) and verify per-tenant sequence integrity

findings.threshold: State rehydration time constant regardless of total event count (snapshot + tail only)
findings.description: SnapshotRehydrationTests explicitly verifies that RehydrateAsync(identity, snapshot) returns ONLY tail events (seq > snapshot.Sequence) and the snapshot state, not the full event stream. The 55-event + seq-50-snapshot case confirms TailEventCount=5 regardless of total history. The bound is structurally guaranteed by EventStreamReader reading from $EventStreamKeyPrefix{snapshot.Sequence+1..current}.

findings.threshold: New tenant/domain via DAPR config store -- no restart, no downtime
findings.description: MultiTenantRoutingIntegrationTests.PostCommands_DynamicTenantAddition_SucceedsAfterReconfiguration demonstrates that registering a previously-unknown tenant+domain after a first failing request makes subsequent requests succeed, with no service restart. Traceability report lists NFR20 as FULL (DynamicConfigurationTests + tenant-hot-add integration).

findings.threshold: Per-tenant 1,000 cmd/min default (configurable); 429 + Retry-After on breach
findings.description: PerTenantRateLimitingTests verifies (a) override threshold honored (premium tenant with PermitLimit=4 throttled at 5th request), (b) tenants without override get default, (c) options validator rejects zero/negative values. RateLimitingIntegrationTests.PostCommands_ExceedsRateLimit_IncludesRetryAfterHeader confirms the 429 + Retry-After contract. CommandsControllerTests.PostCommands_RetryAfterHeader_UsesDeltaSecondsFormat pins Retry-After to delta-seconds format (RFC 7231 compliant).

findings.threshold: Per-consumer 100 cmd/sec default (configurable); 429 + Retry-After on breach
findings.description: PerConsumerRateLimitingTests.RateLimited_Response_IncludesRetryAfterAndBothIds verifies 429 with Retry-After AND ProblemDetails containing both tenantId and consumerId. TenantLimiter_RejectsFirst_WhenTenantLimitIsExceeded verifies chained limiter ordering (CreateChained) and exact Retry-After Delta==60s. Health endpoints bypass rate limiting.

Primarily a performance NFR but has a capacity angle: the integration tests demonstrate serial command acceptance but do not sustain 100 cmd/sec under load. No sustained-load harness exists. Rate-limiting defaults (1,000/min per tenant, 100/sec per consumer) align with the target, but capacity-under-load is not empirically proven. Risk: concurrent-write contention on the same aggregate actor is validated via ActorConcurrencyConflictTests, but throughput at the 100 cmd/sec instance-level aggregate is unmeasured.
recommendations: Add a k6 or NBomber harness that sustains 100 cmd/sec for 60s and captures 429 rate and p99 latency
Coordinate with the performance subagent to share the harness

findings.description: No load test exists for query endpoints. Traceability report lists NFR39 as NONE. The 1,000 queries/sec ceiling is entirely unvalidated.
recommendations: Add sustained-load query harness (1,000 qps for 60s) against read endpoints
Measure p99 latency and error rate at target throughput

findings.threshold: >= 10 concurrent admin UI users without degradation
findings.description: No concurrent-UI test exists. Traceability report lists NFR45 as NONE. The admin UI (Hexalith.EventStore.Admin.UI) has unit/component tests but no multi-user Playwright or SignalR-burst scenario.
evidence: no concurrent admin UI test found
recommendations: Add Playwright test simulating 10 parallel admin sessions browsing projections/aggregates and asserting p95 page-load under target
Validate SignalR hub scale with 10 concurrent subscribers for projection-change notifications

findings.threshold: Aspire-deployable to Docker Compose, Kubernetes, Azure Container Apps -- no custom scripts
findings.description: AppHost/Program.cs supports PUBLISH_TARGET=docker|k8s|aca via AddDockerComposeEnvironment, AddKubernetesEnvironment, and AddAzureContainerAppEnvironment. Deployment guides exist for all three targets. Rated PASS as deployability (horizontal-scale enabler), though replica counts are not parameterized at the AppHost level -- scaling decisions are pushed to the target platform (HPA for k8s, scale rules for ACA).
recommendations: Consider parameterizing eventstore replica count via Aspire WithReplicas(...) for production publish targets once multi-replica tests are added

priority_actions: P0: Add volume test for NFR17 -- 10,000 active aggregate actors with command/query load measuring memory + p99 latency
P0: Add sustained-load command harness for NFR7 -- 100 cmd/sec for 60s with latency + 429 tracking
P0: Add sustained-load query harness for NFR39 -- 1,000 qps for 60s with p99 + error rate
P1: Add multi-replica Aspire test for NFR16 -- 2+ eventstore replicas with actor redistribution assertions
P1: Extend NFR18 multi-tenant test to 10+ concurrent tenants with parallel command dispatch
P1: Add Playwright concurrent-admin-UI test for NFR45 -- 10 parallel sessions
P2: Publish reference HPA/KEDA manifests under deploy/ (currently only documented in guides)
P2: Parameterize eventstore replica count at the Aspire AppHost level for k8s/aca publish targets
summary: Scalability design is structurally sound -- DAPR actors provide consistent-hash placement primitives, snapshot+tail rehydration bounds state reconstruction (NFR19 PASS), rate limiting is fully validated (NFR33/34 PASS with Retry-After delta-seconds), and dynamic tenant hot-add works without restart (NFR20 PASS). However, the headline capacity ceilings (10K aggregates/instance, 100 cmd/sec, 1,000 qps, 10 admin users) are entirely unvalidated empirically -- no volume, load, or multi-replica test exists, and the Aspire AppHost runs a single replica of each service with no parameterized scaling. Recommend a CONCERNS gate until at least NFR17, NFR7, and NFR39 have sustained-load coverage.
